2, 2, 2, 20 to 35. Okay, great. We have
it all around the spectrum. Okay, well,
here's what's nice. Even if you don't
have very many sales people, this is
going to be very helpful for you to
understand. If you have a lot of sales
people, this may already be just a good
starting place for you to build out an
AI agent that does this, but you might
have a big unlock of like, "Oh, there's
other workflows that we can do." And
actually, before I talk about the lead
stuff, let me just explain something to
you.
When it comes to using AI,
I do not think, "Hey, let me give
everyone a license to ChatGPT, Claude,
or whatever the current hotness is, and
just hope that they do it well." That is
not what I'm thinking of at all. I'm
thinking in workflows, okay? So,
how I do this is I go, "What is a
workflow that we're already doing today
that I can use AI to make happen
faster?" So, that's one question to ask
yourself. And here's the other question
to ask yourself. What is
um the What is something that we would
do a lot more of if we could,
but we can't because it takes a lot of
effort, that with AI I can remove the
effort and then we can do way more of
it?" So, an example of that would be a
lot of um
uh like a lot of folks, they'll sell
like $30,000 deals. Okay, let's say
that's the average deal size for you.
And then you get a million dollar deal
that comes through and you're like,
"Holy crap. I'm going to create this
customized material that I'm going to
send them in between the meeting. I'm
going to do whatever I can to try to win
this deal."
You can deliver that quality of an
experience that you would only do for a
really large deal to every deal. And it
puts you in a way better position to be
able to win win that deal, right? These
are the types of things that I want you
to think about. And there's a couple of
things There's a couple of ways to to do
this, but one of the ways I like to do
is I like to look at the frequency,
the vibrational frequency. No, the the
frequency that a task comes up. And then
I also like to look at
the time it takes.
How long does a task take?

>> And how frequent does it come up?

that you have tasks that fit into this
quadrant here. Where it's like, "Hey, so
let's for our purposes, lead research
will be one of those tasks."

size of the text, but you get the idea.
So, what I recommend is write out all of
the tasks that you do and you plot them
based on how long they take you to do
and how frequently they come up. And
what ends up happening is you'll you
might have a task like lead research
here that comes up fairly frequently
and takes a decent amount of time when

about leads real quick. When you're
doing lead research, generally a lead
comes in, right? And then there's a very

is
does it fit our ICP?
Our ideal customer profile, right? And
two outcomes of this
and this is not going to be very um
uh groundbreaking. You're going to
predict what they are right now. Two
outcomes of this, either yes they do,
which in that case,
you want to identify signals, which are
reasons to reach out,
or no,
they don't. And if that's the case, we
want to remove them from the list, okay?
And the reason why I think it's
important for you to understand this
workflow is cuz we need to figure out
where AI can help within this workflow.
I chose lead research cuz it's something
that will improve everybody's sales
efforts. Having more information on a
lead is useful. Um it's very easy to
plug in and get AI to do a lot of the
the task. So, what we would do is we
would hire out a lead researcher. Leads
would come in, however that happens for
them. They might be inbound leads, they
might be leads from a list buy, whatever
it is. They would go through and they
would say, "Hey, is this part of our
ideal customer profile?" And if not,
they would make sure that that lead
never got routed to a salesperson. And
if it was, this is no.
And if it was, which is yes here,
they would do research to figure out,
"Are there any signals?"
And based on those signals,
and sometimes whether they found them or
not, just depends on your process, but
you would
find the signals, and then outreach
would begin. And that outreach might be
a marketing campaign, [clears throat]
it might be um
uh sending to our SDR or an AE, whatever
it might be for you.

lead research under the example I'm
giving you. Now, here's the beauty.

out, and you have AI do parts of it, and

>> okay. So, why did I build it out this
way? One is what I just described, but
the other reason, which is a really
important one, is it serves as a good
foundation that you can plug things
into. You can automatically populate
leads into the spreadsheet. You can also

happen from any starting point, which is
why I like it. You can go to make.com,
pick your starting point, and then do

manual for you as a starting point,

automatically populate the spreadsheet?

this whenever a lead gets entered into
the CRM that has a certain status or
something?" You can do that, too. Also,
can I just send these emails to
instantly and have them go out
automatically? Yeah, you can.
And you can build that out.
Um you can do a lot here.
Can we create different email copy? Can
this be marketing email? Can these be
text messages? Yes, yes, yes, yes, and
yes.
It's nice to have a good baseline of
building your first workflow to
understand how these things kind of work
and connect and then go how do I bake
these in? And I think there's a gap
between people who use AI a lot. There's
There's like the people who use AI a lot
in the
in the chat interface where they're
going back and forth with Claude or
ChatGPT or whatever. And then there's
people who build out workflows that are
business-centered workflows. And this is
actually going to transition me into
something that I think is really
important. When I say business-centered
centric workflows, what I mean is as a
company, as a department, like take the
sales department for example.
As a sales department, we have a lot of
stuff that goes on, right? There's a lot
of things that we that that we do.
And
so much of it becomes tribal knowledge.
Where as sales people we learn like oh
this kind of works in this situation,
this works in this other situation, and
we build this tribal knowledge and it
leaves when the sales rep leaves. So
what do we do? A company of a certain
size, actually if you could comment
here, comment if you have a sales
operations team and or a sales
enablement team. Just say like yes to
both or which which one that you have.
And I'm asking [clears throat] this
because this is what happens as you as
you grow, right? So what happens is you
hire your first sales rep and you're
trying to get all of your tribal
knowledge into that person. You're like
please download my brain. And then you
kind of get them up to speed a little
bit and you're like all right, this
person is working pretty well, they're
starting to figure it out, and then it
starts getting built built a little bit
more a little bit more. You hire another
person doesn't really translate very
well. Then you hire another person and
you're like okay, that translated good
with this person person. At a certain
point a company gets to a size
where they're like okay, we need to
systematize our best practices. We need
to build stronger playbooks. We need to
have a sales operations that kind of are
the glue that make sure that all of the
processes are being followed and that
kind of thing.
And when you're building that, I don't
need to keep sharing here, but I'm going
to come back to this in a minute. Um and
when you are going through that process,
you go, all right, well, let's start
really building out a sales process. You
hire sales enablement, you hire revenue
operations. It doesn't look like anybody
here has those departments, but that's
what happens as you scale. You hire
humans to help make sure that we're
keeping up with the best practices.
Well, what AI gives you the ability to
do is it gives you the ability to own
that best practice.
Because it no longer has to live in the
head of the sales people. We can take it
out of the head of the sales people and
we can put it into a company brain. So,
let me walk you through a little bit
about what um
about this concept of this company
brain.
Um
and really the way that I think about
this is what if your company could
remember everything it's ever learned
about selling?
Very, very important cuz here's what's
interesting about sales. We have sales
calls with prospects, right? We have all
of these sales calls. And a lot of
things come up. Like I was saying, we
calibrate sales people. And by the way,
half of what a sales person knows who
works for you, half of what they know
about how to sell your product or
service is conscious and the other half
is just unconscious. They just calibrate
it cuz they're, you know, emotionally
intelligent people and they kind of
figure things out. Um well, things are
happening on calls. Objections are
coming up. Our reps are responding to
the objections. They're having deep
discovery with folks. They're
quantifying their pain points. All of
these things come up on these calls.
And one of the things at Sales Right
that we're really focused on is
extracting
everything that happens on a sales call
and putting that on the deal level. And
here's why that's important. I want you
guys to be thinking about this. Uh this
directly relates to how you end up
building AI agents. You'll see how this
all comes together in a minute.
Um you have the deals, right? So, all
these things are happening on calls. You
extract the best practices on the calls.
Like, how should we respond to this
objection? What seemed to work? What
seemed to not work? That kind of stuff.
And what's nice about sales is there's a
clear one or lost
for every single deal.
We either won the deal or we lost the
deal.
So, all of these things you extract from
the calls that live in the deal level,
ultimately we get to a place where that
deal is either closed one or closed
lost.
And that allows us to find patterns.
Hey, when we encounter this kind of
objection, if we overcome it like this,
it seems to work and lead us to winning.
Hey, when we do deeper discovery, it
seems that we're more likely to win a
deal.
Hey, when a customer
um
has budget established and the sales rep
did a good job uncovering the budget and
the timeline, we're more likely to win a
deal. We identify these patterns. They
could look like having over four
stakeholders involved in a deal
increases the chance of it closing by
80%. Manufacturing vertical deals go
better when IT is brought in early. When
mid mid-level managers are brought into
a demo, we need to position the product
from their perspective as well. These
are patterns that show you, "Hey, we are
more likely to win it win when we do
this and less likely to win when we do
that." Right? These are the best
practices that you used to build an
enablement team around to capture and
then roll out to other people. Um a big
thing that we're building, the vision
that we have, is taking all of these
patterns and building it into a company
brain. I call this the sales execution
memory layer. Some of this memory, these
best practices, are deal specific. Hey,
for this deal we have to do this thing.
In this vertical we have to do this
thing. Some of them are rep oriented,
right? Like, "Oh, there's a certain way
they like things structured. They like
they like sending emails like this. They
like doing these types of things." At a
certain point, it probably gets bumped
up to a company level best practice.
Hey, we just know like I'll give you a
real example. We had a customer of ours
where if you looked at all of their
deals,
close one deals, had double the amount
of objections per deal than close lost
deals.
And funny enough, the close lost deals
um or excuse me, the close won deals had
a higher um
res- uh percent chance of or not percent
chance, sorry. The average rep handling
of an objection
was higher. So, not only did close won
deals get more objections, they were
also objections that got handled better.
But, then it gets even more interesting.
If you look at the per rep we do all of
this in Sales Thread. If you look at it
per rep, what you find is that their top
performing sales reps have the highest
concern surfacing. So, they surface
cons- concerns out of the prospects
more, which gets more objections out on
the table, which gives them the
opportunity to handle those objections
well, which results in more deals
closing. So, all of a sudden the focus
for the team becomes, "Hey, how do we
make sure all of our sales reps are
surfacing concerns from the prospect?"
And we can see these patterns. And we
used to have to do this by listening to
a bunch of calls. So, if anyone's a
sales manager here, you know, like I
would have to uh listen to so many sales
calls
in order to understand some of these
patterns. And sometimes you just listen
to one and you're like, "Okay, I'm you
know, I'm just going to say that the rep
probably struggles in this area." We're
not quite sure. Sometimes our instincts
are good there, sometimes they're not.
The idea here is let's just get 100%
coverage of AI analyzing every single
call that our sales rep does, pulling
out the best practices,
and then figuring out what seems to be
more correlated to close won, what's
more correlated to close lost. So, that
way we have this company brain that not
only can we use when we're onboarding
new reps or when we're working with with
existing reps so that it's more clear to
them what the best practices are, but
also we can use with AI agents. And
that's what I want to tie this back into
here.
As you have a workflow that works for
you, this is an example one that I've
built out. I'm going to give you the
guide in a minute. But, we have this
workflow here that's built for you.
Right?
As this gets better and you start
understanding your workflow flow more,
the next step becomes building this
company brain. And Sales Thread is
building this for the sales department,
the sales execution memory layer, the
brain of how you sell. And as that
builds up over time, you plug agents
into it. So, we have a suite of AI
agents that we built on top of the Sales
Thread's brain to be able to do work
alongside the sales reps. This is today
how it works. The vision is you can plug
in any agentic system into your brain
because you can use that information to
do work. And I'm trying to show you,
"Hey, this is how we use AI to do work."
Sure, we can all just chat in threads
back and forth, but once you start
building out workflows, once you start
extracting best practices, that's when
things get really, really interesting.
Okay, so I'm going to transition here.

now is coaching, like you can get a

struggling, where the rep is good, and
you can track their improvements in
certain things that you could only track
before by listening to calls. Like,
that's it's killer for that kind of

sense of how likely a deal is to close
because we have a sense of how this deal
is going across all of the sales calls.

whenever they are using AI to get to a
certain output, whether it be an email
or like a
like a promotional email that you want
to send or something like this, just ask
at the very end like, "Hey,
what would it take to get an AI agent to
finish this task for me?" And actually
like publish this email or whatever. And
through APIs, you can probably build
something out. So, anyway, depends on
